timing_authority: tinygrad: ProfileGraphEvent GPU-busy (extra/qk_decode_time_tax_audit.py, owned route + Q4K_GEMV_WARP); llama: rocprofv3 per-family us/token (decode_kernel_trace.json oracle). NOTE tinygrad GPU-busy SUM (~13.7ms) > wall (~11.7ms) due to overlap; llama serial stream ~no overlap (sum==decode_ms).

weight_gemv.note: PARITY -- Q4K_GEMV_WARP closed the FFN GEMV gap; tinygrad ffn_gate_up 2880 + ffn_down 2320 + attn_qo_proj 1360 + attn_kv_proj 400 + lm_head 660 = 7620 ~= llama 7686

attention_compute.note: NEAR-PARITY -- owned tile 434 + combine 229 vs llama tile 342 + combine 116 + streamk 49

kv_cache_copy.note: tinygrad still materializes the full-MAXC canonical store (E_49152 x2); FO2 removed the CAST copy, not the STORE materialization; gqa pays it too

norm_rope_small_residual.note: tinygrad norm_rope_small_ops 3890 - KV copy 1545 = ~2345 genuine norm/rope/reduces vs llama rmsnorm 613 + rope 365 + residual 84 = 1062; tinygrad emits many small UNFUSED kernels

ffn_activation.note: tinygrad ffn_activation (silu/gate, unfused) vs llama ffn_silu 129 + q8_quant 335

summary: Weight-GEMV PARITY, attention NEAR-PARITY. The residual gap is now: (1) KV-copy materialization (+1341us), (2) unfused norm/rope/small-ops (+1283us), (3) unfused FFN activation (+1076us). These are GPU-busy gaps; ~2ms is hidden by overlap (wall gap only +1.73ms). The decode bottleneck has shifted from attention/GEMV to small-op fusion + KV-copy lifecycle.
